Establishes the core concepts, entities, relationships, and constraints that define FirstUK Bank's enterprise structure.
context: The Business Ontology defines the core concepts, entities, relationships, and constraints that govern FirstUK Bank's enterprise.

description: The ontology's scope of defined business entities is quantified by this metric.
context: **Entities Defined:** 30+

description: The ontology's mapped relationship network is quantified by this metric.
context: **Relationships:** 50+

description: The ontology's governance constraints are quantified by this metric.
context: **Constraints:** 100+

Customer and party entities serve as the foundational basis for product issuance, management, and interaction.
context: Party & Customer Domain — People and organizations interacting with the bank

description: Accounts are structured around customer and party entities as primary holders or beneficiaries.
context: Account Domain — Customer accounts and holdings

description: Banking products are conceptually mapped to or realized through specific account structures for delivery.
context: Product Domain — Banking products and services

description: Valid account structures must exist prior to executing money movement and account activity transactions.
context: Transaction Domain — Money movement and account activity

description: Regulatory and risk frameworks dictate customer data handling, identity verification, and interaction standards.
context: Risk & Compliance Domain — Regulatory and risk concepts

description: Compliance rules and risk assessments constrain product design, pricing, distribution, and suitability.
context: Risk & Compliance Domain — Regulatory and risk concepts

description: Account structures are subject to regulatory reporting, KYC requirements, and risk control mandates.
context: Risk & Compliance Domain — Regulatory and risk concepts

description: Transaction processing is strictly bound by fraud detection rules, settlement limits, and regulatory mandates.
context: Risk & Compliance Domain — Regulatory and risk concepts

description: Organizational structure defines the roles, teams, and governance layers responsible for customer engagement.
context: Organization Domain — Organizational structure and roles

description: Organizational roles and committees are established to oversee, enforce, and report on risk and compliance frameworks.
context: Organization Domain — Organizational structure and roles

FirstUK Bank manages the Party banking concept, which encompasses all natural persons and organizations interacting with the bank.

properties.scope: All interacting entities including individuals, businesses, employees, and regulators

context: Any natural person or organization that interacts with FirstUK Bank.

The customer identifier is uniquely mapped to the Party entity for data normalization.
context: customer_id — Unique identifier (linked to Party)

description: A customer maintains a one-to-many relationship with bank accounts.
context: has_accounts → Account (1:many)

description: A customer maintains one or more registered addresses.
context: has_address → Address (1:many)

description: A customer undergoes KYC verification processes as part of onboarding.
context: has_kyc_verification → KYC_Verification (1:many)

description: Customer identity validity is mandated by this business rule.
context: Every customer must have a valid identity (see KYC concept)

description: Customer must pass AML screening before account opening per this rule.
context: Every customer must pass AML screening before account opening

description: Customer's ability to hold multiple accounts is defined by this rule.
context: Customer can hold multiple accounts

description: Customer's joint account arrangements are governed by this rule.
context: Customer can have joint account holders

description: Customer is subject to AML screening processes prior to account activation.
context: Every customer must pass AML screening before account opening

context: UK customers must have a UK address

description: The Address concept is governed by the requirement that addresses must be verified using official documentation.
context: Address must be verified (utility bill, council tax, etc.)

description: The Address concept is governed by the rule enforcing the standard UK postcode format.
context: Addresses use UK postcode format

context: Linked customer attribute in KYC workflow.

context: review_basis — Linked to Bank of England base rate, margin

description: Entity extracted from this document chunk
Entity extracted from this document chunk
Establishes a primary contractual and holder relationship for the account.
context: has_primary_holder → Customer (1:1)

description: Associates the account with a specific banking product offering.
context: has_products → Product (1:1)

description: Maintains a many-to-many relationship where transactions update the account balance.
context: has_transactions → Transaction (1:many)

description: Links the account to recurring payment instructions.
context: has_standing_orders → StandingOrder (1:many)

description: Associates the account with physical or virtual payment cards.
context: has_cards → Card (1:many)

description: Links the account to individual or joint holders managing it.
context: has_account_holders → AccountHolder (1:many, including joint holders)

description: Defines the interest earning mechanism applicable to the account.
context: A contractual arrangement between a customer and FirstUK Bank to hold money, make transactions, and earn interest.

description: Enforces the requirement that every account must have exactly one primary holder.
context: Every account must have one primary account holder (customer)

description: Dictates that the account balance must be derived from the transaction ledger.
context: Account balance is calculated from transaction ledger

description: Restricts the account to maintaining only one operational status at any given time.
context: Account can only be in one status at a time

description: Prohibits the reactivation of accounts once they have been formally closed.
context: Closed accounts are not reactivated

An account must have at least one primary holder established before it can be fully operational.
context: Account must have at least one primary holder

description: The status and establishment of an account holder are strictly governed by the primary holder requirement rule.
context: Account must have at least one primary holder

description: Joint account holders are subject to specific compliance checks and screening obligations under this rule.
context: Both holders must pass KYC/AML

description: Account holders, particularly joint ones, must successfully complete KYC/AML screening before account operations are permitted.
context: Both holders must pass KYC/AML

description: The withdrawal capabilities and liability limits for joint account holders are defined by this authority rule.
context: Either joint holder can withdraw full balance

description: The calculation methodology for interest on the account balance is dictated by this rule, applying to the total balance rather than per holder.
context: Interest calculated on total balance (not per holder)

description: Each account holder record maintains a direct data linkage to its parent account via the account identifier.
context: `account_id` — Linked account

The Joint Account concept is governed by the rule requiring mutual consent from all holders for account closure.
context: Both holders must consent to account closure

description: The Joint Account concept is governed by the rule defining shared financial responsibility for overdraft facilities.
context: Both holders responsible for overdraft

description: The Joint Account concept is governed by the rule permitting transaction initiation by all account holders.
context: Both holders can initiate transactions

description: The Joint Account concept is governed by the rule outlining formal dispute resolution and complaint handling procedures.
context: Dispute resolution per [[departments]] Complaint Handling

description: The Joint Account concept is governed by the rule establishing joint and several liability for all account participants.
context: Both holders jointly and severally liable

description: The Joint Account concept is governed by the rule mandating AML due diligence checks for all account participants.
context: Both included in AML due diligence

description: The Joint Account concept is structurally linked to the Account Holder entity as its constituent participants.
context: holders → AccountHolder (2+ records)

Card status and lifecycle management are governed by lock/unlock control rules.
context: Card can be locked/unlocked by cardholder

description: Replacement procedures for compromised cards follow the stated policy.
context: Lost/stolen cards can be replaced (fee £15)

description: Initial issuance is restricted to a single card per linked account.
context: Only one card per account (initially)

description: Daily spending and withdrawal caps are tracked against this metric target.
context: daily_limit — Daily transaction limit (e.g., £500 for ATM)

description: Contactless payment capabilities are bounded by the specified transaction threshold.
context: Contactless limit: £100 per transaction

Defines the primary account ownership relationship for a transaction.
context: `on_account` → Account (1:many)

description: Identifies the source account for transfer transactions.
context: `from_account` → Account (transfer source, optional)

description: Identifies the destination account for transfer transactions.
context: `to_account` → Account (transfer destination, optional)

description: Links the transaction to the customer who initiated it.
context: `initiated_by` → Customer (1:1, optional)

description: Associates a transaction with a fraud alert when flagged by detection systems.
context: `flagged_by_fraud_detection` → FraudAlert (1:many, optional)

description: Defines Standing Order as a specific transaction type for recurring transfers.
context: Standing Order | Debit | Recurring transfer | Mortgage payment

description: Defines Direct Debit as a specific transaction type for bill payments.
context: Direct Debit | Debit | Bill payment | Utility payment

description: Enforces that transactions are immutable and cannot be deleted to maintain audit trails.
context: Transaction cannot be deleted (audit trail)

description: Defines the mathematical relationship where account balance is calculated as the sum of all associated transactions.
context: Account balance = sum of all transactions

description: Mandates that every transaction must be linked to a fraud risk score for risk assessment.
context: Each transaction linked to fraud risk score

description: Requires comprehensive logging of all transactions to satisfy regulatory compliance standards.
context: All transactions logged for compliance

Standing Order operates on a specific Account, establishing a one-to-many relationship.
context: `on_account` → Account (1:many)

description: Each recurring transfer initiated by the Standing Order automatically generates a corresponding Transaction.
context: `generates_transactions` → Transaction (1:many)

description: The Standing Order's amount and frequency are fixed and cannot be modified directly, requiring cancellation and recreation instead.
context: Amount and frequency cannot change (must cancel and create new)

description: Cancellation of the Standing Order requires a mandatory 30-day advance notice, excluding fraud or account closure scenarios.
context: 30-day notice for cancellation (except fraud/closure)

description: The Standing Order remains active and continues to operate even if the original account holder changes, particularly in joint accounts.
context: Standing order survives account holder changes (if joint)

description: The Account Holder is the initiating party that creates and manages the recurring transfer mechanism.
context: A recurring transfer initiated by the account holder.

Direct Debit is linked to an Account on which payments are drawn, establishing a 1-to-many relationship.
context: `on_account` → Account (1:many)

description: A Direct Debit mandate triggers the generation of one or more Transaction records upon execution.
context: `generates_transactions` → Transaction (1:many)

description: A Customer must exist and authorize the mandate before a Direct Debit can be established.
context: Customer authorizes specific biller via DD mandate

description: The Direct Debit concept is protected and governed by the Direct Debit Guarantee framework.
context: DD protected by Direct Debit Guarantee

description: The Direct Debit process is constrained by rules requiring explicit customer authorization for each biller.
context: Customer authorizes specific biller via DD mandate

description: The Direct Debit concept is constrained by a rule limiting debit amounts to the maximum authorized without prior notice.
context: Biller cannot debit more than maximum amount without notice

description: The Direct Debit lifecycle is constrained by a rule allowing customer cancellation up to 5 days before the scheduled payment.
context: Customer can cancel DD up to 5 days before payment

description: The Direct Debit execution process is constrained by a rule limiting failed payment retries to typically one attempt.
context: Failed DD can be retried (usually once)

Links the KYC verification process to the specific customer being verified in a one-to-one relationship
context: `for_customer` → Customer (1:1)

description: Associates the KYC verification process with multiple identification documents used for validation in a one-to-many relationship
context: `verified_documents` → IdentificationDocument (1:many)

description: The KYC verification concept is constrained by the rule requiring mandatory verification before account opening
context: KYC mandatory before account opening

description: The KYC verification process is subject to a periodic refresh cycle rule specifying the review interval
context: KYC refreshed periodically (every 3–5 years)

description: The KYC verification process must apply Enhanced Due Diligence procedures when triggered by high-risk customer status
context: High-risk customers require Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD)

description: The KYC verification process is governed by a rule mandating the retention of associated documents for a fixed period post-closure
context: KYC documents retained for 6 years post-closure

AML Screening process queries the OFAC Sanctions List to identify potential financial crime matches.
context: Screening Lists Checked: - OFAC Sanctions List (US Treasury)

description: AML Screening process queries the UN Sanctions List to identify potential financial crime matches.
context: Screening Lists Checked: - UN Sanctions List

description: AML Screening process queries the EU Sanctions List to identify potential financial crime matches.
context: Screening Lists Checked: - EU Sanctions List

description: AML Screening process queries HM Treasury Sanctions Designations to identify potential financial crime matches.
context: Screening Lists Checked: - HM Treasury Sanctions Designations

description: AML Screening process queries INTERPOL Red Notices to identify potential financial crime matches.
context: Screening Lists Checked: - INTERPOL Red Notices

description: AML Screening process queries PEP Databases to identify Politically Exposed Persons.
context: Screening Lists Checked: - PEP (Politically Exposed Persons) databases

description: AML Screening process queries Adverse Media Databases to identify negative financial crime news.
context: Screening Lists Checked: - Adverse media databases

target_name: Mandatory AML Screening at Account Opening
description: The AML Screening process is subject to the mandatory rule requiring screening during account opening.
context: Key Business Rules: - AML screening mandatory at account opening

target_name: Ongoing AML Monitoring for High-Risk Customers
description: The AML Screening process is subject to the rule requiring continuous monitoring for high-risk customers.
context: Key Business Rules: - Ongoing AML monitoring for high-risk customers

target_name: Automatic Account Freeze and SAR Filing on Sanctions Hit
description: The AML Screening process is subject to the rule mandating automatic account freeze and SAR filing upon a sanctions hit.
context: Key Business Rules: - Hit on sanctions list = automatic account freeze + SAR filing

target_name: Account Application Rejection on Failed Screening
description: The AML Screening process is subject to the rule requiring account application rejection when screening fails.
context: Key Business Rules: - Failed screening = account application rejected

description: Entity extracted from this document chunk
Defines the mandatory 30-day filing timeline for SAR submissions upon suspicion of financial crime.
context: SAR must be filed within 30 days of suspicion

description: Mandates that the customer must not be informed about the SAR filing to maintain reporting confidentiality.
context: Customer not informed (reporting confidential)

description: Permits and enforces the freezing of accounts pending investigation triggered by the SAR.
context: Account may be frozen pending investigation

description: Imposes regulatory breach consequences as a direct result of failing to file the SAR.
context: Failure to file SAR = regulatory breach

description: Suspicious transactions act as the primary trigger for generating a SAR.
context: `triggered_by_transactions` → Transaction (1:many)

description: AML screening processes must be completed prior to or during the generation of a SAR.
context: `based_on_aml_screening` → AML_Screening (0:1)

description: Links the SAR to the specific customer involved in the suspected financial crime.
context: `for_customer` → Customer (1:many)

description: Acts as the regulatory recipient and oversight body for SAR filings related to suspected financial crime.
context: A report filed with National Crime Agency (NCA) for suspected financial crime.

Fraud alert is linked to the underlying account that generated it.
context: `on_account` → Account (1:many)

description: Fraud alert is optionally linked to the specific transaction involved in the fraud event.
context: `on_transaction` → Transaction (0:1)

description: Fraud alert can be associated with card fraud patterns and unauthorized card usage.
context: `alert_type` — Unauthorized use, Card fraud, Account takeover, Rule violation

description: Fraud alerts are subject to high-risk alert blocking rules that mandate automatic transaction blocking upon detection.
context: High-risk alerts automatically block transaction

description: Fraud alerts trigger customer dispute processes governed by the dispute right rule.
context: Customer can dispute fraud (Fraud Policy)

description: Fraud alerts related to unauthorized use are governed by the reimbursement rule requiring bank repayment within 10 days.
context: Bank reimburses unauthorized fraud within 10 days

description: Fraud alert data generation and lifecycle are governed by the ML feedback loop rule ensuring continuous model improvement.
context: Fraud data feeds ML model for continuous improvement

description: The Fraud Detection ML Model generates and processes fraud alerts when suspicious activity is detected.
context: A system-generated alert when fraud is detected.

description: Fraud alert data is fed back into the ML model to enable continuous improvement and retraining.
context: Fraud data feeds ML model for continuous improvement

description: Financial Crime team members act as reviewers for fraud alerts requiring manual assessment.
context: reviewer — Financial Crime team member (if manual review)

description: The Employee entity is responsible for reviewing Suspicious Activity Reports to ensure regulatory compliance.
context: reviews_sar → SAR (1:many, optional)

Imposes a validation constraint ensuring transaction amounts are strictly positive.
context: Amount > 0 | `transaction.amount > 0`

description: Enforces referential integrity by requiring the transaction account ID to reference a valid account record.
context: Account must exist | `transaction.account_id REFERENCES account(account_id)`

description: Prevents modification of completed or pending transactions after they have been posted.
context: Immutable post-posting | `transaction.status IN (Pending, Completed) AND cannot_edit_completed`

description: Entity extracted from this document chunk

The SAR filing process is governed by a compliance rule mandating submission within 30 days of suspicion.
context: SAR filed within 30 days | `SAR.filing_date <= suspicion_date + 30 days`

description: The SAR filing process performance is measured against the defined 30-day timeframe metric.
context: SAR filed within 30 days | `SAR.filing_date <= suspicion_date + 30 days`

description: KYC verification processes are governed by a compliance rule requiring refresh cycles within 3-5 years.
context: KYC refreshed 3-5 years | `KYC.next_review_date <= TODAY() + 5 years`

description: The KYC verification process performance is measured against the defined review timeframe metric.
context: KYC refreshed 3-5 years | `KYC.next_review_date <= TODAY() + 5 years`

description: The fraud claim and reimbursement process is governed by a compliance rule mandating settlement within 10 days.
context: Fraud reimbursement 10 days | `Fraud_claim.reimbursement_date <= claim_date + 10 days`

description: The fraud reimbursement process performance is measured against the defined 10-day timeframe metric.
context: Fraud reimbursement 10 days | `Fraud_claim.reimbursement_date <= claim_date + 10 days`
